cause_analysis.narrative: Microluxe Studio churned on 2026-02-05 after 184 days with Zoca. Conversation collapsed into a 21-day silence starting 2025-12-17. Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 4 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Retention] Implement a 'cancellation keyword' realtime alert to AMs + Growth team. Today this signal is discovered only post-churn.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 4 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.

[SLA example] On 2026-02-03 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Cancellation of Service Request: Zoca\n\nBody: Hi, I hope you are doing well. My name is Tanvi Mittal , and I a…" — no outbound response in 30 days.

[SLA example] On 2026-02-03 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Re: Cancellation of Service Request: Zoca\n\nBody: Yes please I’m closing my business due a moving of the count…" — no outbound response in 30 days.
